title: Parenting Skills Classes * At Risk Families - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Child Care & Parenting > Parenting Skills Classes * At Risk Families 4 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues African American Community At Risk Families Child Abuse Issues Disabilities Issues Divorce/Custody/Support Issues Domestic Violence Issues Fathers Fetal Alcohol Spectrum Disorders Hispanic/Latino Community Mothers Native American Community Offender/Ex-Offender Issues Sexual Assault Issues Substance Use Disorder Issues Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details EUGENE RELIEF NURSERY THERAPEUTIC EARLY CHILDHOOD PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers classroom and in-home support. Services include help with setting goals, supporting social and emotional development, and creating a safe and nurturing home environment. Children receive developmental screenings. Also provides access to child and family counseling, addiction support, parenting education, a food pantry, a clothing closet, and family social events. Limited transportation available. 1720 W 25th Avenue Eugene, OR 97405 Eligibility: Families with children ages 2-6 who are low income and experiencing multiple stressors. Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm, Friday 8:30am-12:30pm.
